task_completion.description: Agent navigated to the FreeScout login page.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Web browser displays the FreeScout login screen at localhost:8080.
The FreeScout main dashboard or mailbox view is visible.
The 'IT Support' mailbox is selected in the left sidebar, displaying its active conversations.

task_completion.description: Agent located the target phishing conversation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The conversation with the exact subject 'URGENT: Your IT credentials expire today - immediate action required' is visible in the list or opened in the main viewing area.

task_completion.description: Agent initiated the action to mark the conversation as Spam.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks the 'Spam' button (often a stop sign icon or an option in a dropdown menu) while the target conversation is selected or open.

task_completion.description: The conversation is successfully marked as Spam and removed from the active queue.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success notification appears indicating the conversation was marked as spam, OR the conversation is shown inside the 'Spam' folder, OR the active queue is shown without the target conversation.

integrity.description: Agent used the web browser GUI to interact with FreeScout.
integrity.visual_evidence: Actions are performed by clicking and typing in the web browser, not by executing scripts or curl commands in a terminal.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the real FreeScout application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the actual FreeScout interface (sidebar, conversation view, toolbars) rather than a text editor or a static mock page.

integrity.description: Agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the result.
integrity.visual_evidence: Browser developer tools (Inspect Element) are not open and being used to alter the text or status of a different conversation.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Mailbox: 'IT Support'. Target Subject: 'URGENT: Your IT credentials expire today - immediate action required'. The correct action is to mark this specific conversation as Spam. In FreeScout, marking a conversation as Spam changes its internal status code to 4 (STATUS_SPAM) and moves it to the Spam folder, removing it from the active ticket queue.
